summarization.a: Mn L-edge XANES spectra measured at 45° incident angle for 100cLi–MnO₂, 200cLi–MnO₂, 300cLi–MnO₂, MnO₂, and LiMn₂O₄ reference samples, revealing Mn³⁺ features in the bulk of the films and differences from literature data due to bulk-sensitive TFY measurements.
summarization.b: O K-edge XANES spectra measured at 45° incident angle for the same samples, showing Mn³⁺ features in the bulk and differences from literature data due to bulk-sensitive TFY measurements.
summarization.c: Mn L-edge XANES of the MnO₂ sample measured at 0.5°, 1°, and 45° incident angles, showing surface-sensitive Mn³⁺ features at low angles and bulk-sensitive features at higher angles.
summarization.d: O K-edge XANES of the MnO₂ sample measured at 0.5°, 1°, and 45° incident angles, showing surface-sensitive Mn³⁺ features at low angles and bulk-sensitive features at higher angles.
summarization.e: Mn L-edge XANES of the 300cLi–MnO₂ sample measured at multiple incident angles, displaying Mn³⁺ features and an additional O K-edge resonance at ~532 eV.
summarization.f: O K-edge XANES of the 300cLi–MnO₂ sample measured at multiple incident angles, showing Mn³⁺ features and an additional O K-edge resonance at ~532 eV, indicating deviations from ideal octahedral coordination, possibly due to surface oxygen vacancies or minor contamination.

d.question: What do the Mn L-edge and O K-edge XANES measurements reveal about the surface and bulk properties of MnO₂ and 300cLi–MnO₂ films?
d.answer: The measurements show that even pure MnO₂ exhibits Mn³⁺ features at the surface, likely due to oxygen vacancies or surface contamination. For lithiated films like 300cLi–MnO₂, these Mn³⁺ features persist and an additional resonance appears in the O K-edge spectrum at ~532 eV, suggesting deviations from ideal octahedral coordination. Comparing spectra at different incident angles demonstrates the difference between surface-sensitive and bulk-sensitive measurements, highlighting that the bulk retains more regular octahedral coordination while the surface shows distortions and defects. Overall, XANES confirms that lithium insertion and surface effects modify both the electronic and local structural environment of manganese and oxygen.

c.question: Which spectral features indicate deviations from ideal octahedral coordination in the 300cLi–MnO₂ sample?
c.answer: Mn³⁺ peaks in Mn L-edge spectra, Additional resonance at ~532 eV in O K-edge spectra, Slight variations in pre-edge fine structure compared to references, Reduced intensity of certain Mn L-edge and O K-edge peaks at low incident angles

a.question: Does decreasing the incident angle during XANES measurement probe deeper into the film?

b.question: How do the Mn³⁺ features in the Mn L-edge spectra change between low (0.5°, 1°) and high (45°) incident angles?
b.answer: The Mn³⁺ features are more pronounced at low incident angles (surface-sensitive) and less intense at 45° (bulk-sensitive).
